The '''Battle of Iskalon''' was the second major battle in the Nagai invasion of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Battle==
===Buildup===
{{Quote|The longer we've been on this world, the more resistant the natives are growing…|A concerned [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] guard|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 102">[[Star Wars (1977) 102|''Star Wars'' (1977) 102]]</ref>}}
Following the [[Second Battle of Endor]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] traveled to [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], intending to inform the [[Iskalonian]] people of [[Kiro]]'s death, at the hands of [[Den Siva]] on [[Kinooine/Legends|Kinooine]]. Setting down at the wrecked city of [[Pavillion]], the two men found the small platform littered with [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] corpses. The Nagai, under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Kruskob Tiltanka]], had invaded the world and taken the School captive using deadly [[Sonic blaster/Legends|sonic blasters]]. When the Iskalonians refused to build the Nagai base a number had been slaughtered.

===Battle===
{{Quote|You have taken much from the creatures of this world.|[[Kiro]] confronts the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 102" />}}
[[File:Iskalon2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Kiro]] fights underwater.]]

After speaking with the Iskalonian leader, [[Mone]], Luke and Lando learned that Kiro was, in fact, still alive. After escaping Siva on Kinooine, Kiro had returned to Iskalon, and began opposing the Nagai construction efforts on the planet. When the prisoners had been killed, he'd counterattacked and left the littered corpses of the Nagai on the platform for Luke to find. Kiro revealed himself when Skywalker, Calrissian and Mone came under attack from a squad of Nagai. Although he was able to protect Luke and Lando, Kiro was unable to stop Mone, [[Dania Francis]], and their school of Iskalonians from being captured by the Nagai. Kiro left the scene immediately, however, giving Luke no chance to speak to his old friend.

With Pavilion secure, Luke and Lando donned rebreather apparatus and headed underwater, hoping to bring down the Nagai's tower. Luke handed Lando his [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|shoto]] and they began to demolish the structure with [[Luke Skywalker's shoto|Luke's two]] [[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], but were attacked by a patrol squad of Nagai. Once again, Kiro appeared, defeating the Nagai. Kiro then headed inside the tower to try and rescue Mone and the captured Iskalonians, who were being used as slaves by the Nagai. Luke followed him, leaving Lando to demolish the towers foundation.

===Aftermath===
{{Quote|He's going to be right here, protecting his world and his people, as long as they want him to.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] about [[Kiro]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 102" />}}
Although Luke and Kiro were able to free Mone and the others, Kiro was shot in the heart by a Nagai and seemingly killed. Lando brought the tower tumbling into the sea, breaking the Nagai hold on the planet. Kiro then revealed himself to Luke – he had not, in fact, been killed, but feigned death for a second time. He asked Luke to keep his survival a secret, especially from the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] [[Dani/Legends|Dani]], before disappearing into the waters for one final time.
